message:
test: refresh snapshots for custom status support and alphabetic help ordering

Three behavioral changes drove snapshot updates:

1. Custom status support: `br list --status <value>` now accepts any
   status string (returning 0 results for unknown statuses) rather than
   rejecting unrecognized values. The `e2e_list_invalid_status` test is
   renamed `e2e_list_custom_status` with inverted assertions (success +
   "Issues (0)"). The `snapshot_error_invalid_status` snapshot test is
   removed entirely since invalid status is no longer an error path.

2. JSON list output restructured: `br list --json` now returns an object
   `{"issues": [...]}` instead of a bare array, aligning with the schema
   output for other commands. All four list JSON snapshots updated.

3. Help output alphabetized: subcommands in `br --help` are now sorted
   alphabetically rather than by insertion order. All CLI output snapshots
   referencing help text updated accordingly.

4. Graph count field: `e2e_graph_single_issue_honors_toon_env_mode` uses
   `.as_f64().map(|f| f as u64).or(.as_u64())` to handle numeric type
   variance in JSON deserialization for the `count` field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-ykb3","title":"CI Integration: Conformance Test Automation","description":"# CI Integration: Conformance Test Automation

## Purpose
Integrate the comprehensive conformance test suite into CI/CD pipeline to catch br/bd divergence automatically on every PR. This ensures the isomorphism guarantee is maintained throughout development.

## Requirements

### 1. GitHub Actions Workflow

Create `.github/workflows/conformance.yml`:

```yaml
name: Conformance Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
  RUST_LOG: beads_rust=debug

jobs:
  conformance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust (nightly)
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@nightly
        with:
          components: rustfmt, clippy
          
      - name: Cache Cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/bin/
            ~/.cargo/registry/index/
            ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
            ~/.cargo/git/db/
            target/
          key: conformance-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Install Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v4
        with:
          go-version: '1.21'
          
      - name: Install bd (Go beads)
        run: |
          go install github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd@latest
          echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
          
      - name: Verify bd installation
        run: bd --version
        
      - name: Build br (release)
        run: cargo build --release
        
      - name: Run Conformance Tests
        run: |
          cargo test conformance --release -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 2>&1 | tee conformance.log
        
      - name: Generate Test Report
        if: always()
        run: |
          # Parse test output and generate JUnit XML
          cargo test conformance --release -- --format=junit > conformance-results.xml || true
          
      - name: Upload Test Results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: conformance-results
          path: |
            conformance.log
            conformance-results.xml
            target/conformance-logs/
          retention-days: 30
          
      - name: Publish Test Report
        if: always()
        uses: mikepenz/action-junit-report@v4
        with:
          report_paths: 'conformance-results.xml'
          fail_on_failure: true
          include_passed: true

  benchmark-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    needs: conformance
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          # Same setup as conformance job
          
      - name: Run Benchmark Comparison
        run: |
          cargo run --release --bin br-bench -- \
            --format=markdown \
            --baseline=.github/benchmark-baseline.json \
            --fail-on-regression=15% \
            > benchmark-report.md
            
      - name: Comment PR with Results
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const report = fs.readFileSync('benchmark-report.md', 'utf8');
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              body: report
            });
```

### 2. Test Result Reporting

**JUnit XML Generation**:
```rust
// In tests/conformance/report.rs
pub fn generate_junit_xml(results: &[TestResult]) -> String {
    let mut xml = String::from(r#"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>"#);
    xml.push_str(&format!(
        r#"<testsuite name="conformance" tests="{}" failures="{}" time="{}">"#,
        results.len(),
        results.iter().filter(|r| !r.passed).count(),
        results.iter().map(|r| r.duration.as_secs_f64()).sum::<f64>()
    ));
    
    for result in results {
        xml.push_str(&format!(
            r#"<testcase name="{}" classname="conformance" time="{}">"#,
            result.name,
            result.duration.as_secs_f64()
        ));
        if !result.passed {
            xml.push_str(&format!(
                r#"<failure message="{}"><![CDATA[{}]]></failure>"#,
                result.error_message.as_deref().unwrap_or(""),
                result.diff.as_deref().unwrap_or("")
            ));
        }
        xml.push_str("</testcase>");
    }
    
    xml.push_str("</testsuite>");
    xml
}
```

**Summary PR Comment**:
```markdown
## 🧪 Conformance Test Results

| Category | Passed | Failed | Skipped |
|----------|--------|--------|---------|
| CRUD | 56/56 | 0 | 0 |
| Dependencies | 29/29 | 0 | 0 |
| Sync | 32/32 | 0 | 0 |
| Utility | 39/39 | 0 | 0 |
| **Total** | **156/156** | **0** | **0** |

### ⏱️ Performance Summary
- Average test duration: 0.45s
- Total suite time: 2m 15s
- Slowest test: `test_sync_1000_issues` (12.3s)

<details>
<summary>View detailed log</summary>

[Full log output here]

</details>
```

### 3. Failure Handling

On conformance failure, CI must:

1. **Capture full outputs**:
```bash
# Save both br and bd outputs
mkdir -p artifacts/failures/$TEST_NAME
cp br_output.json artifacts/failures/$TEST_NAME/
cp bd_output.json artifacts/failures/$TEST_NAME/
```

2. **Generate visual diff**:
```rust
fn generate_diff_html(br: &str, bd: &str) -> String {
    // Use similar-asserts or custom diff for colored output
}
```

3. **Create actionable error**:
```
❌ Conformance failure: test_create_with_priority

Expected (bd):
  {"title": "Test", "priority": 1, "status": "open"}
  
Actual (br):
  {"title": "Test", "priority": 1, "status": "Open"}  // Note: "Open" vs "open"
  
Difference:
  - status: "open"
  + status: "Open"
  
Suggestion: Check src/model/status.rs for case sensitivity in serialization
```

4. **Archive comprehensive logs**:
```
artifacts/
├── conformance.log          # Full test output
├── conformance-results.xml  # JUnit XML
├── failures/
│   └── test_create_with_priority/
│       ├── br_output.json
│       ├── bd_output.json
│       ├── diff.html
│       └── test.log
└── timing/
    └── performance.csv
```

### 4. Performance Gate (Optional)

**Regression Detection**:
```yaml
- name: Check for Performance Regression
  run: |
    NEW_MEDIAN=$(jq '.summary.median_speedup' benchmark-results.json)
    OLD_MEDIAN=$(jq '.summary.median_speedup' .github/benchmark-baseline.json)
    
    REGRESSION=$(echo "scale=2; ($OLD_MEDIAN - $NEW_MEDIAN) / $OLD_MEDIAN * 100" | bc)
    
    if [ $(echo "$REGRESSION > 15" | bc) -eq 1 ]; then
      echo "::error::Performance regression detected: ${REGRESSION}%"
      exit 1
    fi
```

### 5. Detailed Logging Infrastructure

**Log Levels**:
```rust
pub enum LogLevel {
    Error,    // Only failures
    Warn,     // Failures + warnings
    Info,     // + test progress
    Debug,    // + command details
    Trace,    // + raw outputs
}
```

**Structured Log Format**:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-17T10:00:00.123Z",
  "level": "INFO",
  "test": "conformance_create_basic",
  "phase": "execute",
  "message": "Running br create",
  "context": {
    "command": ["create", "Test issue", "--json"],
    "workspace": "/tmp/conformance_abc123/br_workspace"
  }
}
```

**Log Aggregation**:
```rust
pub struct TestRunSummary {
    pub total_tests: usize,
    pub passed: usize,
    pub failed: usize,
    pub skipped: usize,
    pub total_duration: Duration,
    pub slowest_tests: Vec<(String, Duration)>,
    pub failures: Vec<TestFailure>,
}
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. [ ] Create GitHub Actions workflow file
2. [ ] Add JUnit XML reporter to conformance tests
3. [ ] Configure artifact collection
4. [ ] Add PR comment bot for results summary
5. [ ] Implement failure diff generation
6. [ ] Add benchmark gate (optional)
7. [ ] Document CI requirements in CONTRIBUTING.md
8. [ ] Add status badge to README

## Files to Create/Modify
- `.github/workflows/conformance.yml` - Main workflow
- `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml` - Benchmark comparison (optional)
- `.github/benchmark-baseline.json` - Performance baseline
- `tests/conformance/report.rs` - JUnit XML generation
- `README.md` - Add CI badge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI runs on every PR
- [ ] Clear pass/fail status visible in PR checks
- [ ] Detailed logs available as downloadable artifacts
- [ ] JUnit XML integrates with GitHub test reporting
- [ ] Failure messages are actionable (show diff, suggest fix)
- [ ] README has CI status badge
- [ ] Documentation for local CI reproduction

## Notes
- Tests run with `--test-threads=1` to avoid parallelism issues with bd
- Consider separate workflow for nightly comprehensive runs
- Cache Cargo and Go dependencies for faster CI","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:33.510001730Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:40.875661117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:40.875661117Z","close_reason":"Overlaps with beads_rust-na7 (CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions) which is in_progress","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-ykb3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-egz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ykm","title":"where Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br where` command to show the location of the beads directory and database. Useful for debugging path issues and understanding project structure.

## CLI Interface
```
br where [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --db                      Show database path only
  --jsonl                   Show JSONL directory only
  --config                  Show config file path only
  --all                     Show all paths (default)
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Path Discovery
```rust
fn discover_paths() -> Result<BeadsPaths> {
    let beads_dir = discover_beads_dir()?;  // Walk up to find .beads/
    let db_path = beads_dir.join(format\!("{}.db", get_prefix()?));
    let jsonl_dir = beads_dir.clone();
    let project_config = beads_dir.join("config.yaml");
    let user_config = dirs::config_dir()
        .map(|d| d.join("br/config.yaml"));
    
    Ok(BeadsPaths {
        beads_dir,
        db_path,
        jsonl_dir,
        project_config,
        user_config,
    })
}

fn discover_beads_dir() -> Result<PathBuf> {
    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?;
    let mut dir = cwd.as_path();
    
    loop {
        let beads = dir.join(".beads");
        if beads.is_dir() {
            return Ok(beads);
        }
        dir = dir.parent()
            .ok_or_else(|| BeadsError::NotInBeadsProject)?;
    }
}
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable (default)
```
Beads Project Paths:
  .beads directory:  /home/user/project/.beads
  Database:          /home/user/project/.beads/bd.db
  JSONL directory:   /home/user/project/.beads
  Project config:    /home/user/project/.beads/config.yaml (exists)
  User config:       /home/user/.config/br/config.yaml (not found)
```

### --db (single path)
```
/home/user/project/.beads/bd.db
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "beads_dir": "/home/user/project/.beads",
  "db_path": "/home/user/project/.beads/bd.db",
  "jsonl_dir": "/home/user/project/.beads",
  "project_config": "/home/user/project/.beads/config.yaml",
  "project_config_exists": true,
  "user_config": "/home/user/.config/br/config.yaml",
  "user_config_exists": false
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Discover .beads directory by walking up from cwd
- [ ] Show database path
- [ ] Show JSONL directory path
- [ ] Show config file paths with existence check
- [ ] Individual path flags (--db, --jsonl, --config)
- [ ] Error if not in a beads project
- [ ] Human and JSON output

## Unit Tests
- Finds .beads in current directory
- Finds .beads in parent directory
- Error when not in beads project
- Each path flag outputs single path
- JSON contains all paths
- Existence flags correct

## Dependencies
- Configuration system (for paths)

## Rationale
The where command helps users and scripts locate beads resources. Essential for debugging "command not found" style issues and for scripts that need to operate on beads files directly.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:20:01.621547488Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:39:58.239708508Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:39:58.239708508Z","close_reason":"Duplicates of beads_rust-nct (where Command) which has more detail","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ynn","title":"EPIC: Documentation & Onboarding Excellence","description":"# Documentation & Onboarding Excellence

## Background & Rationale

Based on research of developer experience best practices and the patterns observed in mature tools (xf, cass, beads_viewer, ACFS), br needs comprehensive documentation that enables both human developers and AI coding agents to use it effectively.

### Why This Matters
- No README.md currently exists (critical gap)
- AI agents rely heavily on AGENTS.md for context
- One-liner installation needs clear quick-start docs
- Agent integration requires specific guidance
- Users need troubleshooting resources

## Goals
Create world-class documentation that enables zero-friction onboarding for both human users and AI coding agents, with clear examples and comprehensive reference material.

## Deliverables

### 1. README.md (Project Landing Page)
- One-liner installation command
- TL;DR section (30-second overview)
- Feature table with status indicators
- Quick example (practical usage)
- Architecture overview (high-level)
- Agent integration blurb
- Contributing guide link
- License

### 2. AGENTS.md Enhancement
- Current file is good but needs:
  - More examples of common agent workflows
  - Robot mode flag documentation
  - Error handling guidance for agents
  - MCP integration instructions (when available)

### 3. Installation Guide (docs/INSTALLING.md)
- All installation methods:
  - One-liner script
  - Homebrew
  - Scoop (Windows)
  - Cargo install
  - From source
- Platform-specific notes
- Proxy configuration
- Troubleshooting common issues

### 4. CLI Reference (docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md)
- Every command with examples
- All flags and options
- JSON output schemas
- Exit codes
- Environment variables

### 5. Agent Integration Guide (docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md)
- Supported AI coding agents
- Configuration for each agent type
- Robot mode flags reference
- JSON output parsing examples
- Workflow examples (ready → claim → work → close)
- Error handling patterns
- MCP server setup (when available)

### 6. Troubleshooting Guide (docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md)
- Common errors and solutions
- Database recovery
- Sync conflict resolution
- Performance issues
- Debug logging

### 7. Architecture Documentation (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- Module overview
- Data flow diagrams
- SQLite schema
- JSONL format specification
- Extension points

## Documentation Standards

### Structure per file
1. Quick summary (1-2 sentences)
2. Table of contents for long docs
3. Examples for every concept
4. Links to related docs
5. Last updated date

### Code Examples
- All examples must be tested/working
- Include both human and JSON output
- Show error cases too

### Agent-Friendly Formatting
- Use consistent markdown headers
- Keep paragraphs concise
- Use tables for reference data
- Include JSON schemas where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- README.md exists with all sections
- AGENTS.md enhanced with agent workflows
- All docs pass markdown lint
- Examples are tested and work
- Docs are discoverable (linked from README)
- Search-friendly (good headings, keywords)

## References
- xf README structure
- cass documentation patterns
- Anthropic Claude Code docs
- Rust API documentation guidelines

## Dependencies
- None (can start immediately)
- Complements all other work","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:49:04.481507069Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:46:43.966168675Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:46:43.966102440Z","close_reason":"All documentation deliverables complete: README.md (259 lines), AGENTS.md (392+ lines enhanced), docs/INSTALLING.md (new, 450+ lines), docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md (933 lines), docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md (505 lines), docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md (944 lines), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (675 lines). All docs follow standards: TOC, examples, agent-friendly formatting.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
